note: The world runs on its own clocks, not the player's. Cyclical events keyed to the eight-season calendar (early-spring, late-spring, early-summer, late-summer, harvest, early-winter, deep-winter, thaw; 45 days each, 360-day year) and to specific places. Arriving anywhere means arriving IN THE MIDDLE OF SOMETHING. Plus the framing that makes the population self-driven — people pursuing their own passions on their own timelines, not standing by as quest-dispensers. Engine tie: the world-tick checks the current season/day against these cycles and surfaces what's happening where; the GM narrates the place as alive. Authored by Aevi (PO), from Erik's living-world direction.
principle: A place is not a stage that waits for the player to walk on. It has a calendar, a livelihood, a rhythm of festival and hardship, and people mid-pursuit of their own wants. The player arrives DURING things: a harvest, a mourning, a market-high, a feud's flare, a courtship, a crisis that started before them and continues after. The diversity and the extremes are the engine of story — a passionate baker and a grieving warden and a scheming buyer, all pursuing their own arcs, collide into narrative without a quest ever being 'given.' The GM should be able to answer 'what's happening here right now' from the season and the place, always.

seasonalCycles.world: Thaw's end; planting, first markets, the year's hope. Births and beginnings. The mood lifts.

places.millbrook: Planting Days — the whole village in the fields; a good time to be owed a favor, a bad time to ask for hands
places.greywater_stilts: The Waking of the Marsh — channels reopen, the talking-circle sets the year's terms, newcomers weighed
places.the_wend: The road-people move again; the first caravan-weddings of the year

seasonalCycles.world: Growth, courtship, the Game's season opens in the cities. Energy high.

places.harmonic_heights_terrace: The Resonance Trials — the Heights' great Game-season; standing won here shapes the year, and Ledda's old chord is renewed
places.radiant_plateau_edge: The Kindling — lumens re-lit citywide in a festival of light; a courtship season under the Blaze's foothill
places.thornwake_glade: The Green Quickening — the Glade at its most alive and most welcoming; the Accord's yearly renewal, this year in doubt

seasonalCycles.world: Trade peaks; the passes are open; travel and ambition. The world's busiest turning.

places.kestrels_roost: The High Market — the year's great trade-fair; the Kestrel's ledger is fullest and most vulnerable; the Patient Buyer moves in the crowd
places.tumbledown_market: The Churn's Making-Feast — inventors demonstrate the year's wonders, most of which work once
places.the_redline: The Season of Challenges — the Marches' formal duels decide standing and law for the year

seasonalCycles.world: Heat, tension, the first shortages of a bad year showing. Feuds flare.

places.echo_river_crossing: The water runs lowest and grayest — the crisis at its most visible; tempers between communities at their worst
places.the_gralloch: The badland's cruelest season; the feuds peak; the thing under the violence stirs
places.the_stillhold: The Silent Days — the peace-city's most sacred and most brittle festival; the buried truth closest to the surface

seasonalCycles.world: The great gathering; plenty or its failure; the year judged. Gratitude and reckoning.

places.millbrook: Harvest Home — the biggest festival of the Valley year; a failed harvest here is a catastrophe that ripples to every arc
places.salt_meadows: The Salt-Cutting — the meadows' one busy season, the year's salt laid in
places.the_flesh_temple: The Trials of the Body — the Temple's yearly feats-festival, devotion made spectacle

seasonalCycles.world: Battening down; the passes closing; stories and debts called in before the dark. Inward turn.

places.kestrels_roost: The Last Caravans — the desperate rush over the pass before snow; Sable's disappearance-season; debts settled before the freeze
places.sunken_choir: The Choir sings loudest in the first cold — the returning voices strongest; Vesper's hardest season
places.the_middle_way: The Winter Discourses — body and mind schools debate through the long evenings

seasonalCycles.world: Survival; isolation; the world small and close and honest. What holds, holds; what doesn't, breaks.

places.old_switchback: The pass is shut; Greta's waystation is a lifeline or a tomb; travelers stranded together
places.the_cogitarium: The minds soar while the bodies fail hardest — the Cogitarium's deadliest season, and its most brilliant
places.dw_the_moot: The Deep Deliberation — the Ents' slow argument runs on, indifferent to the season that means nothing to them

seasonalCycles.world: The breaking; floods, mud, released tension, grief for what winter took. The year's rawest turn.

places.the_hollow_march: The Thaw-Deaths — the Ashwardens tend winter's toll; Palework's busiest, saddest season
places.greywater_stilts: The Flood — the marsh at its most dangerous and most alive; the Stilts prove their independence or don't
places.dw_the_burnscar: The green returns fiercest to the scar — the wound most present, the grudge most raw

recurringTroubles.effect: cascades: Millbrook empties toward the Stilts and Roost, prices rise everywhere, the cities' offers to the free places sweeten and sharpen, every arc's pressure ticks up. A living-world consequence, not a scripted event.
the northern map (Roost, Switchback, Churn) partly seals; travel there costs more and risks more; the Churn Reach is nearly cut off until thaw.

a festival is the perfect stage for an arc to erupt — an assassination at the High Market, a truth spoken at the Silent Days, a challenge at the Season of Challenges. The GM should collide cycles with arcs.

populationDoctrine.principle: People are driven, passionate, and pursuing their OWN things — and sometimes just people. Not quest-dispensers. Every named NPC already has wants/fears; the living-world layer says: they pursue those wants ON THEIR OWN TIMELINE whether or not the player helps. The baker is trying to win the Making-Feast; the warden is fighting the Accord's failure; the buyer is buying. The player walks into lives already in motion.
guidance: An NPC met twice should have MOVED — advanced their own want, suffered a setback, changed. The world-tick should let key NPCs progress their arcs offscreen (ties to worldtick + the superstructure).

Not everyone has a quest. Some people are just people — texture, warmth, a good meal, an opinion, a life being lived. The mundane makes the dramatic land. A world of only quest-givers is dead; a world of mostly-just-people with a few burning arcs is alive.

Diversity and extremes drive narrative: a place of passionate opposites (a Redliner and a Stillholder, a Vivimancer and an Ashwarden, a Builder and a Dawnkeeper) generates story by friction, no quest required. Populate for CONTRAST.
Motivation over function: describe a person by what they WANT, not what they offer the player. The offer emerges from the want when the player's path crosses it.
Let people have off-days, moods, distractions. A blacksmith too grief-struck to work today is more real than one perpetually available. Reality over convenience.
populationDoctrine.engineTie: The world-tick advances key NPC wants offscreen (a progress counter per tracked NPC arc), so returning to a place shows change. Generated NPCs (SNG-020) get a want on creation so even procedural people are driven. Flagged to the spec backlog as a light world-tick addition.
populationDoctrine.universalRoles: Every person fills a loose UNIVERSAL ROLE (mediator/warrior/warden/keeper/maker/seeker — see universal_roles.json) expressed through their locale's method. This is HOW to populate for contrast: same role + opposite disposition = built-in friction and story. A traveled-role (a life-warden tending the Cogitium's neglected bodies) is instant narrative — the role carried to a need its homeland can't meet. Describe a person by role+method+want, never by what they offer the player.

gmMandate: The GM can always answer 'what is happening here, right now, that has nothing to do with me' from: the season's cycle for this place + who's mid-pursuit of what + any arc-pressure currently here. Lead scenes with the place being alive; let the player's business intersect a world already in motion. Never a waiting stage.
